title: Admin Interface for Flagged Transactions Review
description: The system shall provide an interface for system administrators to review and act on flagged transactions.
rationale: To enable timely investigation and action on suspicious activity, ensuring the integrity of the system.
business_value: Improves security by allowing quick response to potential fraud, enhancing overall system reliability.

source.description: As a system administrator I need an interface to review and act on flagged transactions So that I can investigate suspicious activity and take appropriate actions to maintain system integrity

acceptance_criteria: All transactions flagged by the system are displayed with relevant details.
The system updates the transaction status upon selecting an action (approve/deny/investigate).
The flagged transaction is removed from the pending review list after updating its status.
preconditions: An admin user is logged in.
The user is on the flagged transactions review page.
postconditions: The selected action (approve/deny/investigate) has been applied to the transaction.
The transaction's status has been updated accordingly.
The transaction is no longer displayed in the pending review list.
functional_constraints: Filtering and sorting capabilities must be provided for the list of flagged transactions.
security_requirements: Access to the flagged transactions interface should be restricted to system administrators only.
Sensitive information about transactions should be handled with encryption and access control measures.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system shall respond within [timeframe] seconds when displaying flagged transactions and processing user actions.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system shall be available at least 99.5% of the time during business hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to handle an increase in the number of flagged transactions by [percentage or number].
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, and the interface should provide feedback on successful actions.

edge_cases: What happens when there are no flagged transactions?
How does the system handle network issues while updating transaction statuses?
What if an admin user tries to access the interface without being logged in?
dependencies: Fraud detection/system rules for identifying flagged transactions
Authentication and authorization service for managing admin users

interfaces.name: Flagged Transactions Review Page
interfaces.description: The user interface where system administrators can view, filter, sort, and take actions on flagged transactions.

interfaces.name: Transactions Database
interfaces.description: The database that stores transaction data and flags identified by fraud detection/system rules.

test_cases.description: Admin user views flagged transactions list with relevant details.
test_cases.expected_result: All flagged transactions are displayed with their relevant details, including transaction ID, amount, timestamp, and flag reason.
The transaction status is updated to 'Approved', and the transaction is removed from the pending review list.
The transaction status is updated to 'Denied', and the transaction is removed from the pending review list.
The transaction status is updated to 'Investigating', and the transaction remains in the pending review list.

test_cases.description: Admin tries to access the interface without being logged in.
test_cases.expected_result: Access denied, and a login prompt appears.
